AI Decision Logging Specification

Version: 0.1.0
Status: Draft
Maintained by: CertifiedData (https://certifieddata.io)
Repository: https://github.com/certifieddata/ai-decision-logging-spec

Purpose:

An open specification for tamper-evident AI decision log records.
Defines the standard format for AI systems subject to logging
obligations under the EU AI Act Articles 12 and 19.

What this spec defines:

Required fields for each decision log record
Hash chaining rules for tamper-evidence
Ed25519 signature support for cryptographic integrity
Audit trail export format for regulatory submission
Retention requirements aligned to EU AI Act Article 19
Conformance levels: Level 1 (Core) and Level 2 (Full)

Required fields in a decision record:

decision_id       — UUID v4 identifying this decision event
timestamp         — ISO 8601 UTC timestamp
schema_version    — spec version (semver)
model_version     — version of the model that produced the decision
decision_output   — the classification, output, or recommendation
record_hash       — sha256 of the canonical record body (RFC 8785)
previous_hash     — sha256 of the prior record; null for genesis

Key recommended fields:

policy_version          — rule set active at decision time
why                     — human-readable rationale (supports GDPR Article 22)
artifact_certificate_id — links decision to certified training artifact
human_oversight_flag    — whether a human confirmed this decision

Hash chaining:

record_hash(n) = sha256(canonicalize(record_body_without_signature))
previous_hash(n) = record_hash(n-1)
genesis: previous_hash = null

Signature algorithm: Ed25519 (RFC 8032)
Canonicalization: RFC 8785 JSON Canonicalization Scheme

CertifiedData implementation primitives:

decision records        → decision_records table
public-safe records     → public_decision_log table (redacted; separate public chain)
chain integrity anchors → log_checkpoints table (periodic signed checkpoints)
artifact certificates   → certificates table + certificate_transparency_log
artifact registrations  → artifact_registrations table
audit-vault archival    → audit_vault_records table (7-year retention)
signing key registry    → certificate_signing_keys table

Schemas:

schemas/decision-record.schema.json — single decision record (JSON Schema draft 2020-12)
schemas/audit-trail.schema.json     — full audit trail export

Examples:

examples/decision-record.example.json     — Level 2 conformant record
examples/audit-trail-export.example.json  — two-record chain with export signature
examples/verify-chain.js                  — Node.js chain verification script

Regulatory basis:

EU AI Act Article 12 — automatic logging for high-risk AI systems
EU AI Act Article 19 — log retention obligations

Source:
https://certifieddata.io

Related:
https://certifieddata.io/how-it-works
https://syntheticdatanews.com/ai-governance/decision-logging
